cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs CREATE zero-byte create benchmark onto a single wrapper that stores create success, created address, and created code size.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark CREATE with zero-byte initcode and value 1.

RPC mapping: a single funded deployed wrapper performs one value 1 CREATE, then stores create success, the returned child address, EXTCODESIZE(child) and child balance as deterministic witness fields.
Only zero-byte variants are admitted; non-empty/max-code-size variants remain blocked until their code-size witness contracts are mapped.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed create success, nonzero child address, zero child code size and child balance are deterministic final observables.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs CREATE zero-byte create benchmark onto a single wrapper that stores create success, created address, and created code size.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark CREATE with zero-byte initcode and zero value.
RPC mapping: a single funded deployed wrapper performs one zero value CREATE, then stores create success, the returned child address, EXTCODESIZE(child) as deterministic witness fields.
Only zero-byte variants are admitted; non-empty/max-code-size variants remain blocked until their code-size witness contracts are mapped.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed create success, nonzero child address, zero child code size are deterministic final observables.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs CREATE non-empty non-zero data create benchmark onto a single wrapper that stores create success, created address, created code size, and created code hash.
cases.upstream_ref: tests/benchmark/compute/instruction/test_system.py::test_create[opcode=CREATE-variant=0.25x max code size with non-zero data]
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark CREATE with 6144-byte non-zero initcode that deploys 6144-byte child code.

RPC mapping: a single deployed wrapper performs one zero value CREATE, then stores create success, the returned child address, EXTCODESIZE(child), and EXTCODEHASH(child) as deterministic witness fields.
Only the smallest non-zero-data and zero-data non-empty variants are admitted; larger code-size ratios and value-bearing non-empty variants remain blocked until separate witness contracts are mapped.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed create success, nonzero child address, child code size, and child code hash are deterministic final observables.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs CREATE2 collision benchmark onto a single wrapper that deploys a proxy, calls it once to create an empty child, then calls it again with the same CREATE2 salt to observe collision failure.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark CREATE2 collision behavior with empty initcode.
RPC mapping: a single wrapper deploys a proxy, calls it once to perform CREATE2 with salt 0 and empty initcode, then calls the same proxy again with the same salt so the second CREATE2 collides.
The proxy call gas is capped at 100000 so the collision exhausts only the inner frame while the outer wrapper retains gas to store witness slots.
CREATE collision remains blocked because upstream depends on mutable pre-allocation of future CREATE addresses, which the current RPC-only harness cannot reproduce.

Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed proxy deploy success, first create result, child code size, collision call failure, and collision returndata size are deterministic final observables.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs RETURN benchmark onto a self-call wrapper that stores inner-call success, returndata size, and returndata digest for the 1024-byte non-zero variant.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark RETURN with a 1024-byte non-zero returndata payload.
RPC mapping: a single deployed contract self-CALLs into its child path so the outer wrapper can observe the inner result without requiring multi-contract orchestration in the manifest.
The child path fills the first 1024 memory byte(s) with 0xff before RETURN.

Wrapper witness contract stores the inner-call success bit in slot0, RETURNDATASIZE in slot1, and KECCAK256(returndata) in slot2; the outer transaction receipt remains successful so receipt_status stays directly observable.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed returndata digest/size are deterministic final observables for the success path.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs RETURN benchmark onto a self-call wrapper that stores inner-call success, returndata size, and returndata digest for the 1024-byte zero variant.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark RETURN with a 1024-byte zero returndata payload.
RPC mapping: a single deployed contract self-CALLs into its child path so the outer wrapper can observe the inner result without requiring multi-contract orchestration in the manifest.
The child path RETURNs 1024 byte(s) from zero-initialized memory.

Wrapper witness contract stores the inner-call success bit in slot0, RETURNDATASIZE in slot1, and KECCAK256(returndata) in slot2; the outer transaction receipt remains successful so receipt_status stays directly observable.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed returndata digest/size are deterministic final observables for the success path.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs SELFDESTRUCT created-child benchmark onto a single persistent wrapper that creates a child, calls it so SELFDESTRUCT executes in the same transaction and stores final-state witness fields.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark SELFDESTRUCT for a created child with zero value under cancun semantics.

RPC mapping: a single wrapper creates a child whose runtime is CALLER; SELFDESTRUCT, calls it in the same transaction, then stores create success, child address, call success, and post-call child code size.
The created-child scenario is admitted because same-transaction create plus SELFDESTRUCT has deterministic final-state evidence under Cancun without requiring traces or mutable prestate.
Existing-account SELFDESTRUCT variants use a separate setup/execution wrapper; initcode SELFDESTRUCT variants remain blocked until their CREATE-result semantics are mapped separately.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed child address, call success, post-call code size, and optional beneficiary balance are deterministic final observables.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs SELFDESTRUCT created-child benchmark onto a single persistent wrapper that creates a child, calls it so SELFDESTRUCT executes in the same transaction and stores final-state witness fields.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark SELFDESTRUCT for a created child with value 1 under cancun semantics.

RPC mapping: a single wrapper creates a child whose runtime is CALLER; SELFDESTRUCT, calls it in the same transaction, then stores create success, child address, call success, and post-call child code size.
The created-child scenario is admitted because same-transaction create plus SELFDESTRUCT has deterministic final-state evidence under Cancun without requiring traces or mutable prestate.
Existing-account SELFDESTRUCT variants use a separate setup/execution wrapper; initcode SELFDESTRUCT variants remain blocked until their CREATE-result semantics are mapped separately.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed child address, call success, post-call code size, and optional beneficiary balance are deterministic final observables.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs SELFDESTRUCT existing-account benchmark onto a single persistent wrapper that first creates and stores a persistent child in setup mode, then calls the stored child in execution mode and stores final-state witness fields.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark SELFDESTRUCT for an existing child with zero value under cancun semantics.
RPC mapping: a single persistent wrapper uses two ordinary invocations: setup mode CREATE2s and stores a child whose runtime is CALLER; SELFDESTRUCT, then execution mode CALLs the stored child.
Setup mode stores setup_create_success, child address, and EXTCODESIZE(child) before execution; execution mode stores call success, post-call EXTCODESIZE(child), and optional beneficiary balance.
The existing-account scenario is admitted because post-Cancun SELFDESTRUCT preserves pre-existing code, making setup/execution lifecycle evidence deterministic without traces or mutable prestate.
Initcode SELFDESTRUCT variants remain blocked until their CREATE-result semantics are mapped separately.

Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed setup create success, child address, pre/post child code sizes, call success, and optional beneficiary balance are deterministic final observables.

RPC mapping: a single persistent wrapper uses two ordinary invocations: setup mode CREATE2s and stores a child whose runtime is CALLER; SELFDESTRUCT, then execution mode CALLs the stored child.

The existing-account scenario is admitted because post-Cancun SELFDESTRUCT preserves pre-existing code, making setup/execution lifecycle evidence deterministic without traces or mutable prestate.
Initcode SELFDESTRUCT variants remain blocked until their CREATE-result semantics are mapped separately.

Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed setup create success, child address, pre/post child code sizes, call success, and optional beneficiary balance are deterministic final observables.
